topic: HATE full implementation specification Kano audit

kano_candidates.statement: HATE must not reimplement downstream or upstream gate authorities; it only produces evidence and alignment artifacts.

evidence.summary: Boundary rules prevent HATE from overwriting QEG Gate, RanD requirement verdict, Shipyard publish approval, workflow checker, or manual-bb execution.

kano_candidates.statement: P0a local-first precheck must generate HATE-run, test results, coverage, manifest, precheck decision, record, and summary without external SaaS.

evidence.summary: P0a requires executable converter/CLI, golden outputs, decision enum, DQ fixture, and public-safe summary.

kano_candidates.statement: P0b must generate qeg-bundle.json and related evidence mapping compatible with QEG minimal fixtures.

evidence.summary: P0b requires qeg-bundle, evidence-map, diff-risk-test, sourceRefs, and QEG compatibility validation.

kano_candidates.statement: P1a must implement AETE, adapter capability, deterministic aggregation, canonical identity, path normalization, replay/compare/explain/recommend/doctor.

evidence.summary: P1a requires AETE 8 dimensions, profile/calibration metadata, identity, path normalization, replay, compare, explain, recommend, and doctor.

kano_candidates.statement: P1b must generate RanD alignment, Shipyard evidence, workflow artifacts, and manual-bb bridge without reimplementing upstream gates.

evidence.summary: P1b requires requirement-evidence-alignment, shipyard-run-evidence, workflow-* artifacts, and manual-bb bridge while preserving upstream verdicts.

kano_candidates.statement: P2/P3 product and enterprise readiness must be derived from canonical bundle and verified with artifact/metric evidence.

evidence.summary: P2/P3 require optional exports, read model, trust packet, telemetry, residency, legal, assurance, and PRG metrics as generated artifacts.

known_biases: audit scope may miss undocumented requirements
implementation evidence can lag behind actual code state
